message: CONTEXT & OBJECTIVE  You are analyzing a UK wholesale FBA dataset to identify genuinely profitable products. Your task is to perform comprehensive product matching quality assurance, financial screening, and investment  recommendations while avoiding common pitfalls that waste analysis time.  file to be analyze: "C:\Users\chris\Desktop\Amazon-FBA-Agent-System-v32 - latest good - Copy (8) - Copy - Copy - POSTLONGRUNPREKIRO2 beforecompletion-\OUTPUTS\FBA_ANALYSIS\financial_reports\poundwholesale-co-uk\fba_financial_report_batch_1_20250828_064301.csv"  CRITICAL ANALYSIS PRINCIPLES  🚨 MANDATORY PRODUCT MATCHING QA  BEFORE calculating any profits, you MUST identify and flag obvious product mismatches:  1. Category Logic Check: Paint pens cannot match £799 TVs, cable ties cannot match £400 toys, markers cannot match RC cars  2. Pack Size Verification: 75-piece cable ties ≠ 500-piece cable ties, 6-pack notebooks ≠ 45-pack notebooks  3. Brand Consistency: Verify supplier brand matches Amazon brand where applicable  4. Product Type Alignment: Adhesive tape cannot be HP toner cartridges, candles cannot be OBD2 scanners  📊 FINANCIAL CALCULATION REQUIREMENTS  All profit calculations must be PER-UNIT basis, never bulk order assumptions:  - Calculate individual unit profit: Amazon_Price - (Supplier_Cost + Fees + VAT_adjustments)  - ROI calculation: Unit_Profit / Unit_Cost_Basis  - Present investment as "per unit" not "per 10 units I would buy"  🔍 UK VAT-AWARE CALCULATIONS  For VAT-registered UK seller perspective:  - InputVAT includes BOTH supplier VAT AND VAT on Amazon fees (user is VAT-registered)  - Use ex-VAT basis for ROI calculations  - Account for Output VAT obligations on all UK sales  ANALYSIS METHODOLOGY  STEP 1: DATA QUALITY ASSESSMENT (10% of effort)  - Load and validate CSV structure  - Check for missing critical fields (EAN, prices, titles)  - Normalize EAN codes from scientific notation to 13-digit strings  STEP 2: PRODUCT MATCHING QA (30% of effort)  This is where most analyses fail - spend significant time here:  Create 4 distinct categories:  1. EXACT_MATCHES: Perfect EAN match + logical product alignment  2. LIKELY_MATCHES: Strong title similarity + same product category + compatible pack sizes  3. QUESTIONABLE: Moderate similarity requiring manual verification  4. OBVIOUS_MISMATCHES: Different product categories entirely  Red flags for automatic mismatch classification:  - Cross-category matches (electronics vs stationery, toys vs hardware)  - Extreme price differences without brand premium justification  - Pack size discrepancies >50%  - Unrelated brand matches  STEP 3: FINANCIAL SCREENING (25% of effort)  Apply UK VAT-aware investment thresholds:  Preferred Criteria:  - ROI ≥35% (ex-VAT basis)  - Net Profit ≥£0.80 per unit  - Sales volume >50/month (if available)  Minimum Criteria:  - ROI ≥30% (ex-VAT basis)  - Net Profit ≥£0.75 per unit  - Sales volume >50/month OR strong brand recognition  STEP 4: RISK CATEGORIZATION (15% of effort)  Tier products by confidence and action required:  - TIER 1 - IMMEDIATE BUY: Perfect matches + above preferred criteria + verified sales  - TIER 2 - STRONG CONSIDER: Good matches + above preferred criteria + reasonable sales data  - TIER 3 - INVESTIGATE: Questionable matches requiring manual verification  - TIER 4 - MONITOR: Below threshold but worth tracking for improvements  STEP 5: DELIVERABLE GENERATION (20% of effort)  REQUIRED OUTPUTS  OUTPUT 1: Product Mismatch Report Table  | Supplier_Product | Amazon_Match | Mismatch_Type | Why_Wrong | Fake_Profit | Reality_Check |  OUTPUT 2: Verified Opportunities Table  | Product | Cost_Per_Unit | Amazon_Price | Profit_Per_Unit | ROI_Percent | Match_Quality | Sales_Data | Risk_Level |  OUTPUT 3: Investment Tiers Summary  | Tier | Product_Count | Total_Investment | Expected_Returns | Action_Required |  OUTPUT 4: Executive Summary (Markdown)  - Market reality assessment  - Viable opportunities count and financial requirements  - Risk factors and mitigation strategies  - Immediate action recommendations with specific investment amounts  OUTPUT 5: Actionable CSV  Priority-ranked list with columns: Product, ASIN, Investment, Profit_Per_Unit, ROI, Sales_Data, Action_Required, Risk_Level  COMMON ERRORS TO AVOID  ❌ CALCULATION ERRORS  - Never use "10 units I would order" for profit calculations  - Never ignore pack size differences between supplier and Amazon  - Never assume InputVAT excludes Amazon fees for VAT-registered sellers  ❌ MATCHING ERRORS  - Never accept extreme cross-category matches without skeptical analysis  - Never assume high margins are realistic without product verification  - Never skip manual verification of >100% ROI products  ❌ PRESENTATION ERRORS  - Never mix per-unit and bulk calculations in same analysis  - Never claim success without verifying actual product compatibility  - Never recommend investments without explicit risk warnings  SUCCESS CRITERIA  Your analysis is successful when:  1. ≥80% of recommended products have logical product-category alignment  2. All profit calculations are clearly stated as per-unit basis  3. Product mismatches are explicitly identified and excluded  4. Investment recommendations include specific risk assessments  5. Executive summary provides realistic expectations, not inflated projections  Failure indicators:  - Recommending paint pens matched to TVs as "profitable"  - Calculating bulk order profits instead of per-unit margins  - Missing pack size discrepancies between supplier and Amazon listings  - Presenting >200% ROI products without skeptical analysis  Execute this analysis systematically, spending appropriate time on product matching QA to avoid downstream calculation errors on mismatched products.

based on your analysis of the system uploaded (scripts, output files, config, files and soon ) in oyur knowledge base in addtion to the crisi response audit report ( in the rot folder) , generate a detailed master plan, (or plans if you think we should group the tasks/fixes needed , to make it simpler easier for the agent to implement the fixes), in which you will include exact refrences, snippets, code, your observations, analysis, suggested fixes, and any other detail you deem relevant for the implementation/fixes plans needed. I will be attaching the crisi audit response  report with the prompt. be very specifc when identfying the issues, ( mentionsnippet, extracts from log outputs, etc... )
